summary: RBAC permission matrix expansion (iteration 153) verified end-to-end. Backend: 10/10 pytest tests pass on /api/roles confirming 12 categories, 71 module keys, 0 orphan labels, all 19 newly-added keys present, admin/CEO/System Admin role auto-backfilled with full {view,create,edit,delete}=true on new keys, non-admin roles backfilled with default false, PUT /api/roles/{id} persists toggles. Regression: /api/auth/me unaffected. Frontend: Tenant Settings → Roles tab renders all 12 categories with correct count badges (Core 10, Reports 5, Operations 7, Tools 5, Documents 2, Requests 3, Marketing 2, Organization 8, Admin 3, Distribution 10, Production 13, Task Management 3); expanding Production lists all 13 modules with toggles checked for Admin role; Distribution lists Stock Dashboard + Cost Cards; Admin lists Tenant Settings/API Keys/Replace SKU. Sidebar shows new items (Account GOP Metrics, Neck Tag Designer, Invoices, Performance Tracker, Investor Dashboard, Meeting Minutes) — all with proper moduleKey gating.
critical_code_review_comments: roles.py list_roles() backfill mutates and persists on every GET — fine for low cardinality but causes a write per role that lacks a key on each call until convergence. Consider gating by 'len(missing) > 0' (already done) and adding an index on (tenant_id, id) for the update_one to keep it cheap.

models/role.py is approaching 330 lines with three large permission dictionaries (DEFAULT/MANAGER/VIEWER). Acceptable but consider extracting MODULE_CATEGORIES + MODULE_LABELS + DEFAULT_MODULE_PERMISSIONS into models/role_modules.py for readability if it grows further.

VIEWER_PERMISSIONS is generated via comprehension giving Viewer view=True on EVERY new module key (including production/admin ones). The post-hoc disable for tenant_settings/team is fine but new admin-ish keys (api_keys, sku_replace, rejection_cost_config, etc.) silently grant view to Viewer role. Consider adding them to the Viewer denylist.

DashboardLayout.js: Sales context defines 'Tasks & Requests' once but Production/Distribution/Marketing contexts each redefine it — keys consistent now, but a single shared constant would prevent future drift.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 153 RBAC expansion is fully validated. Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_153_rbac_modules.py covers categories/keys/backfill/persistence and is regression-safe. UI verified at /tenant-settings → Roles tab via Playwright (login as CEO surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth/test123). Suggestion above re: VIEWER_PERMISSIONS auto-granting view on admin-style new keys (api_keys, sku_replace, rejection_cost_config) is the only follow-up worth considering — not a regression, just a least-privilege observation.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature addition, not a bug fix. Implementation matches spec: routes/roles.py list_roles() backfills missing keys per role on read, models/role.py defines 71 keys across 12 categories with comprehensive labels, DashboardLayout.js declares moduleKey on every sidebar item including renamed lead_scoring_model and newly-keyed production/admin/distribution items.
